steps.title: Understand what makes a solution
steps.content: A point $(x, y)$ satisfies the system if it makes BOTH inequalities true simultaneously. We'll test each choice by substituting its coordinates into both inequalities.

steps.title: Test each choice by substitution
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing Choice A — $(0, -12)$ $(0, -12)$:
First inequality: $-12 \le 0 + 4 = 4$ ✓
Second inequality: $-12 \ge -2(0) - 6 = -6$? No, $-12 \lt -6$ ✗
Fails.

Testing Choice B — $(0, 12)$ $(0, 12)$:
First inequality: $12 \le 0 + 4 = 4$? No, $12 \gt 4$ ✗
Fails immediately.

Testing Choice C — $(-12, 0)$ $(-12, 0)$:
First inequality: $0 \le -12 + 4 = -8$? No, $0 \gt -8$ ✗
Fails immediately.

Testing Choice D — $(12, 0)$ $(12, 0)$:
First inequality: $0 \le 12 + 4 = 16$ ✓
Second inequality: $0 \ge -2(12) - 6 = -30$ ✓
Both inequalities are satisfied. That matches Choice D — $(12, 0)$.
